mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which immediately boosts equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles produce negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing discount rates and compressing equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5 are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings potential, generating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns with a t-stat below 1.5 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of instances when INDPRO drops below the -1.0% threshold.
